data.title: Insperity: Margin Progress Offsets Softer Volume as Outlook Cuts WSE Growth, Supporting Hold Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Andrew Nicholas has maintained their neutral stance on NSP stock, giving a Hold rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Andrew Nicholas has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors relating to both growth and profitability. While Insperity exceeded expectations on gross profit and adjusted EBITDA thanks to disciplined cost control and slightly better benefit costs, earnings per share were constrained by a higher tax rate and the overall narrative remains one of stabilization rather than clear acceleration.

At the same time, management reduced its outlook for worksite employee growth, now anticipating a modest contraction driven by weaker new sales, elevated client attrition, and timing issues around net hiring, even as the mix shift and expense discipline allow the company to maintain its full-year EBITDA guidance. This balance of solid margin progress against softer volume trends and a more challenging demand backdrop leads Nicholas to see limited near-term upside in the shares, supporting a Hold rather than a more constructive rating.

In another report released today, Truist Financial also reiterated a Hold rating on the stock with a $35.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Asure Software: Buy Rating Reiterated on Strong 2026 Start and Platform Momentum; $11 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
In a report released today, Bryan Bergin from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on Asure, with a price target of $11.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bryan Bergin has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Asure’s strong start to 2026 with revenue and adjusted EBITDA exceeding expectations and a slight increase in full-year growth guidance while maintaining margin targets. He also highlights accelerating organic growth, a sizable contracted backlog with near-term conversion potential, and stable client behavior with no clear signs of macro-driven weakness.

In addition, Bergin points to the strategic benefits of migrating most customers to the Asure Central platform, which should support higher cross-sell activity, rising product attach rates, and meaningful PEPM expansion over the next few years, aided by newer offerings like AsureWorks. Despite recent share outperformance in a generally out-of-favor HCM sector and ongoing AI-related sentiment swings, he views the current valuation of under 2x CY27E EV/S as presenting an attractive risk-reward, and he keeps his price target unchanged at $11.

In another report released today, Lake Street also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $13.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: TriMas Maintained at Buy as Analyst Reaffirms $45 Price Target on Strong Balance Sheet and Earnings Power
data.description:
BWS Financial analyst Hamed Khorsand has maintained their bullish stance on TRS stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hamed Khorsand has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including TriMas’s strong balance sheet and earnings power. The company exited the first quarter of 2026 with a substantial net cash position and has two business units that are expected to each contribute meaningfully to adjusted EBITDA by 2027, underscoring a solid long‑term earnings profile.

TriMas also exceeded his revenue expectations in the latest quarter, with part of the upside tied to tooling sales that should translate into future project revenue, reinforcing forward visibility. In addition, shares currently trade at an enterprise value that appears low relative to the company’s projected adjusted EBITDA and anticipated cost-driven margin improvements, while ongoing share repurchases and potential strategic interest from acquirers further support his unchanged $45 price target and Buy view.

data.title: Atlassian’s Renewed Cloud Momentum and AI Monetization Upside Support Reiterated Buy Rating and Unchanged $150 Price Target
data.description:
Canaccord Genuity analyst David Hynes has maintained their bullish stance on TEAM stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Hynes has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Atlassian’s renewed growth and emerging AI monetization. He points to a clear inflection in cloud performance, with faster revenue growth, improving net retention, and strong early traction from AI features like Rovo, where users are expanding their spend roughly twice as fast as non‑users, supported by the scalable Teamwork Graph and increasing MCP activity.

He also highlights Collections as a powerful revenue driver, noting robust adoption in both Teamwork and Service offerings, broadening use cases beyond IT, and measurable operational benefits for customers from AI‑driven automation. Combined with a solid backlog, durable mid‑20% cloud growth, nearly 30% free cash flow margins, and a valuation of roughly 3x EV/revenue for a business capable of 20% growth at scale, he views the current price as attractive and thus reiterates a $150 target and Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, KeyBanc also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $130.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on Federal Realty After Strong Q1, Raised 2026 FFO Guidance and Robust Leasing Momentum
data.description:
Analyst Juan C. Sanabria of BMO Capital maintained a Buy rating on Federal Realty, with a price target of $120.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Juan C. Sanabria has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Federal Realty’s stronger‑than‑expected first‑quarter performance, driven by higher occupancy, term fees, and cost efficiencies. He also highlights robust leasing momentum with double‑digit rent spreads, which is translating into healthy same‑store net operating income growth and supports a favorable multi‑year earnings outlook.

Additionally, Sanabria notes that management raised 2026 FFO guidance above prior levels and Street expectations, even after factoring in a brief dip before recently signed leases begin contributing later in 2026 and into 2027. He believes that redevelopment and capital recycling are adding accretive growth on top of solid underlying fundamentals, and that the company’s high‑quality demographic footprint should prove defensive and attractive amid broader macroeconomic uncertainty.

data.title: Five9 Earns Buy Rating on Surging AI Subscription Growth, Strong CCaaS Momentum and Enhanced Transparency
data.description:
William Blair analyst Arjun Bhatia has reiterated their bullish stance on FIVN stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Arjun Bhatia has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including a stronger-than-expected quarter in which subscription revenue growth ticked higher, supported by solid conversion of backlog. He also views the rapid acceleration in AI subscription revenue, now a meaningful share of total subscriptions, as evidence that Five9 is successfully capturing rising demand for AI-driven contact center solutions.

In addition, Bhatia highlights management’s new, more detailed disclosures, which enhance transparency around AI and core CCaaS trends and should help investors better track performance. He also notes that core CCaaS revenue growth remains steady, underpinned by healthy seat expansion, robust upsell and cross-sell activity, and a long runway as on-premises contact centers migrate to the cloud and increasingly adopt AI.

In another report released today, Northland Securities also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $47.00 price target.
